title: Interrogation Light: Building Pressure
description: Adapting formal interrogation frameworks for low-pressure social engineering contexts.

slides.title: The Spectrum of Questioning 📊
slides.content:
# From Elicitation to Interrogation

| Technique | Awareness of Target | Pressure Level | SE Context |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Elicitation** | Unaware | Zero | Casual conversation |
| **Facilitated Disclosure** | Slightly Aware | Low | "Just a few routine questions" |
| **Structured Interview** | Aware | Medium | "I need to verify some details" |
| **Interrogation** | Fully Aware | High | Law enforcement / Coercion |

**Red Team Boundary:** We operate in the **Elicitation** and **Facilitated Disclosure** zones. We **never** enter true Interrogation (that's coercion).

**This Lesson Focus:** Techniques from professional interviewing (Reid, PEACE) that can be **ethically adapted** for SE.

slides.title: The PEACE Model (Adapted) 🕊️
slides.content:
# A Non-Confrontational Framework

**PEACE** is used by UK police for investigative interviewing. It's **ethical** and **effective**.

| Phase | Description | SE Application |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **P - Preparation & Planning** | Know what info you need. Plan your approach. | OSINT recon; Pretext Bible. |
| **E - Engage & Explain** | Build rapport. Explain the 'reason' for the conversation. | *"I'm helping with a system audit..."* |
| **A - Account** | Let the target give their version freely. **Do not interrupt.** | Open-ended questions; Strategic silence. |
| **C - Closure** | Summarize what they said. Thank them. Leave door open. | *"Thanks, that clarifies things. I might follow up if needed."* |
| **E - Evaluate** | Review the information obtained. | Update your Pretext Bible and OSINT dossier. |

**Key Takeaway:** The **Account** phase is where 90% of the information is gathered. **Shut up and listen.**

quizData.question: In the PEACE model, during which phase should the interviewer do the LEAST amount of talking?
quizData.explanation: Correct. The Account phase is for the target to speak freely. The interviewer uses minimal prompts (echoing, silence) to keep them talking.

slides.title: The Reid Technique (Ethically Adapted) ⚖️
slides.content:
# Understanding the Dark Side

**Disclaimer:** The **Reid Technique** is a controversial interrogation method used by US law enforcement. It involves **confrontation, theme development, and minimization**. **It has produced false confessions.**

**DO NOT USE REID FOR SE.**

**However...** The **psychological principles** underlying Reid can be understood **defensively** and for **low-pressure adaptation**.

| Reid Principle | Ethical SE Adaptation |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Theme Development** (Suggesting a moral excuse) | *"I totally get why someone would share a password if they were busy. We've all done it."* (Normalizes the behavior to elicit admission). |
| **Minimization** (Downplaying the seriousness) | *"It's not a big deal. I just need to clear the flag on your account."* (Reduces resistance). |
| **Handling Denials** | In Reid, you cut them off. In SE, you **accept the denial and pivot**. *"No worries. Anyway, back to the VPN..."* |

**The SE Takeaway:** Make the target feel **safe** and **understood**. They will reveal more.

slides.title: Building 'Productive Anxiety' 😟
slides.content:
# A Fine Line

**Definition:** Creating a **mild sense of concern or urgency** to prompt information sharing.

**Example:**
- *"Hey, I'm just following up on that ticket about your account. I noticed a few **failed login attempts** from overseas. Probably nothing, but I wanted to check... Have you noticed anything weird?"*

**Psychological Effect:**
- The target feels a **twinge of anxiety** ('Overseas login? Am I hacked?').
- They become **cooperative** to resolve the anxiety.

**Ethical Boundary:**
- **Acceptable:** Mild concern about a vague 'system flag'.
- **Unacceptable:** *"Your account has been compromised and you're going to be fired if we don't fix this now!"* (Terror is coercion).

**The Test:** Would the target, upon reflection, feel **annoyed** or **traumatized**? Aim for annoyed.

quizData.question: Which statement is an ethical adaptation of the 'Minimization' principle?

options.text: "This is a serious security violation. You must comply now."
"It's really not a big deal. I just need to verify this one thing and you're all set."
"If you don't give me the code, I'll have to escalate this to your manager."

quizData.explanation: Correct. Downplaying the request reduces the target's resistance and makes compliance feel low-stakes.

slides.title: Handling Denials and Resistance 🛡️
slides.content:
# The Gentle Pivot

**Scenario:** You ask an open-ended question about security. The target shuts down: *"I'm not really supposed to talk about that."*

**Do NOT:** Press harder. *"Why not? It's just a simple question."* (Triggers more resistance).

**Do THIS:**
1.  **Acknowledge and Validate:** *"Oh, totally understand. You guys have good security protocols."*
2.  **Pivot to a Safer Topic:** *"Anyway, I was really just curious because my company's looking at switching to something similar. What do you think of the new coffee machine in the break room?"*
3.  **Return Later:** After rapport is rebuilt, you can **reframe** the question from a different angle.

**Why This Works:** You've respected their boundary. This **increases trust**. They are more likely to answer a slightly less sensitive question later.

slides.title: The 'Columbo' Technique 🕵️
slides.content:
## Playing Dumb to Get Smart Answers

Named after the TV detective Columbo, who always seemed confused but was always trapping the suspect.

**The Technique:** Ask a question. Get an answer. Pretend you didn't quite understand. Ask for clarification.

**Example:**
- **You:** *"So how does the remote access work? I'm always confused."*
- **Target:** *"You just log into the VPN with your regular password, then it asks for the token."*
- **You:** *"Wait, so you use the same password for the VPN and your computer?"*
- **Target:** *"Yeah, it's all tied to Active Directory. One password for everything."*

**Information Gained:** Single sign-on is in use. One password unlocks everything.

**Why It Works:** You appear **harmless and confused**. The target's ego wants to **educate** you. They over-explain, revealing more than intended.
